title: Dense Crispy Spanish Suckling Pig Paella
description: Spicy, crisp Valencian dish featuring black treacle, deglazed fond, and ayurvedic-friendly tamasic ingredients. Traditionally served at dinner time.
instructions: Cut the pork shoulder into large chunks and set aside.
Heat 2 tbsp of vegetable oil in a large paella pan over medium heat.
Add the Spanish onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for another minute.
Add the chorizo and cook until browned, about 3 minutes.
Add the pork shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the pimenton, smoked paprika, cinnamon, bay leaves, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the rice and stir to coat with the spice mixture.
Add 2 cups of fond deglazed and bring to a boil.
Add 1 cup of chicken broth and stir to combine.
Cover the pan with a lid and transfer to the oven.
Bake for 20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove from the oven and let rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Heat 2 tbsp of olive oil in a small pan over medium heat.
Add the saffron and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Strain the saffron oil into a small bowl and set aside.
Slice the paella into large pieces and serve with the saffron oil, fresh parsley, and lemon wedges.
Garnish with Valencian saffron if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
